engineering.translation: Indeed (hi), supreme happiness (sukham uttamam) approaches (upaiti) this yogi of peaceful mind (praśānta-manasam), in whom rajas has been calmed (śānta-rajasam), who has become Brahman (brahma-bhūtam), free of stain (akalmaṣam). 6.27 names the result-state. The yogi praśānta-manas (peaceful-minded) and śānta-rajas (in whom the rajas-guna has been calmed) attains supreme happiness; the precondition is brahma-bhūtam and akalmaṣam. Shankara: rajas is the cause of attachment and aversion; when calmed, the yogi has become Brahman, free of every form of stain. Ramanuja: when rajas (the source of mental activity) has been rendered calm, supreme happiness arises naturally. Per chapter-6 thesis: the engineer with restless effort calmed isn't slow — they're settled-in-action. Engineering analog at narrowed operational scope.
engineering.concrete_scenario: A staff engineer at year twelve, observing herself at year three. Year three: she shipped a redesigned authentication service backed by Postgres; the launch consumed a stressful sprint of seventeen-hour days; the rollout produced a memorable incident still cited in retros; the conversion metric, by any measurement, came out excellent. She had produced rajasic output — quality forged through restless effort. Year twelve: she is rolling a redesigned permission-check service via GitHub Actions, structurally more intricate than the year-three project. Her workdays span eight hours. The Datadog dashboards remain green at deploy. The launch numbers, again by any reckoning, look excellent. Externally the year-twelve cadence appears slower-paced. Internally it carries identical intensity at lower noise — the rajas has settled; the practice has compounded; the same problem-class yields clean artifacts without urgency-spikes. She is not less effective per workweek; she is more effective per hour spent, because the wind that drove seventeen-hour days has been removed at the source. Contrast the engineer at year twelve still grinding seventeen-hour days, still triggering rollout incidents, still confusing restlessness with rigor. Scoped operationally, the verse is precise: śānta-rajas does not mean slowness. It means settled-in-action. STRETCHED at metaphysical scope.
engineering.falsifiability: The analog fails if a reader reads śānta-rajas as low-energy or disengagement. Rajas calmed is not rajas absent; the yogi is not lethargic. The engineering analog: the senior whose effort is calibrated rather than urgent. An engineer who reads this verse as a licence for low-output has misread.
engineering.counter_example: When the engineer's apparent settledness is in fact tamasic inertia — the slowness comes from disengagement rather than calmed effort — the verse does not name this. Tamas resembles śānta-rajas externally and is its opposite operationally. The chapter elsewhere distinguishes the gunas; the engineering reader must too.
engineering.implication: Audit whether your apparent intensity is producing better output, or merely louder process. Where the rajas has truly calmed, the output sustains at the same level with less noise. Where the noise has merely been suppressed, the output drops.
engineering.quotable_line: Rajas calmed is not effort absent; the engineer is settled-in-action, not slowed.

iterations.mutation: v0 generation: BG 6.27 names the rajas-calmed result-state. Engineering analog at narrow scope: settled-in-action vs slowness. Shankara/Ramanuja preserved. Counter-example distinguishes śānta-rajas from tamasic inertia. Confidence MEDIUM at operational scope; STRETCHED at metaphysical scope.
